data.title: Morgan Stanley’s Katy Huberty sees tactical pause to European equities’ rebound
data.description:
Investors should “expect choppiness this earnings season” for European equities (VGK), (IEV), according to Katy L. Huberty, global director of research at Morgan Stanley.

While the firm remains constructive on the medium-term outlook, Huberty anticipates a tactical pause to the rebound at the index level as investor sentiment has become overextended.

The strategist points to elevated expectations around geopolitical developments as a key factor behind the expected pause.

According to Huberty, investor sentiment, “particularly around expectations for a resolution to ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, has come a long way,” suggesting the market may have priced in too optimistic a scenario.

Despite the anticipated choppiness at the index level, Huberty sees favorable conditions emerging for individual stock selection.

 

 

 

The strategist notes that consensus earnings growth for MSCI Europe (IEUR) overall is moving up, and stock-level dispersion is currently “well above seasonal averages,” making for a “strong stock-picking environment” as the market shifts back to idiosyncratic drivers amid earnings season.

Looking at sector-specific expectations, Morgan Stanley identifies clear winners and losers heading into the reporting period.

According to the strategist, the firm expects “the biggest skew to beats in the energy, utilities, banks, and telecom sectors,” positioning these industries as potential outperformers during the current earnings cycle.

Conversely, Huberty warns that certain sectors face a more challenging earnings hurdle.

The strategist expects “the biggest skew to misses in luxury, autos, and staples,” according to Morgan Stanley’s analysis, suggesting investors may want to approach these industries with greater caution as companies report their quarterly results.

European-focused ETFs: (VGK), (EZU), (BBEU), (IEUR), (HEDJ), (IEV), (FDD), (SPEU), and (FEP).

data.title: Ball upgraded, Graphic Packaging downgraded at Raymond James
data.description:
Ball Corp. (BALL) won a vote of confidence from Raymond James on Tuesday after analyst Matt Roberts upgraded the shares to Outperform from Market Perform, arguing the company’s beverage can business continues to benefit from durable industry trends.

Roberts said beverage cans appear better insulated than other packaging categories from broader demand weakness tied to inflation and shifting consumer spending.

“We believe BevCans are particularly immune to broader volume concerns in other substrates/categories or, at the least, relatively less impacted by potential indirect demand impacts from inflation,” Roberts wrote in a note to clients.

The analyst also pointed to Ball’s relatively modest exposure to Asia and the Middle East, potentially limiting geopolitical and regional demand risks compared with more globally exposed industrial names.

In addition, Roberts said higher input costs for aluminum, energy and freight are largely passed through to customers on a spot basis, helping protect margins even in a volatile cost environment.

Raymond James set a $73 price target on Ball (BALL) shares, implying roughly 14% upside from the stock’s previous close.

Graphic Packaging cut as pricing power wanes

In the same sector call, Raymond James downgraded Graphic Packaging Holding (GPK) to Underperform from Market Perform.

Roberts said paper-based packaging markets remain challenged, with weak supply-demand dynamics limiting the company’s ability to raise prices.

“Applicable paper substrates have lacked any pricing power to date given unfavorable supply and demand conditions,” he wrote.

The contrasting calls suggest Raymond James sees a more favorable setup in metal packaging, where pricing structures and category resilience may offer steadier returns than paper packaging markets facing oversupply pressure.

data.title: OpenAI launches Images 2.0, allowing users to generate multiple images at once
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) introduced ChatGPT Images 2.0 on Tuesday, which improves on the quality of the images, reduces the error rate, and allows users to generate multiple images at the same time.

"Images 2.0 is a huge step forward," said OpenAI CEO Sam Altman during a Tuesday livestream. "It's like going from GPT-3 to GPT-5 all at once. Its ability to make extremely beautiful things is remarkable. The team really cooked with this one, and we can't wait to see what you'll do with it."

The model is more deliberate with where it places texts and images in the design, OpenAI said. There is an Instant and a Thinking version of the model.

"When a thinking model is selected in ChatGPT, Images 2.0 can search the web for real-time information, create multiple distinct images from one prompt, and double-check its own outputs," the company said. "With thinking, the model can take on even more of the heavy lifting between idea and image, especially when accuracy, up-to-date information, consistency, and visual cohesion matter most."

Images 2.0 is available today for all users in ChatGPT, Codex, and the API.

data.title: Trump nominates Christopher Phelan to head the Council of Economic Advisers
data.description:
President Donald Trump nominated Christopher Phelan to be the chairman of the Council of Economic Advisers, replacing Stephen Miran, who resigned when his stint as a Federal Reserve governor extended past February.

Phelan is a  professor of economics at the University of Minnesota and an adviser to the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis's Research Department, the district bank said on its website.

The administration submitted Phelan's nomination on Tuesday, according to a statement.

Phelan received his AB and AM degrees in economics and computer science from Duke University and the University of Chicago. In 1990, he received a PhD in economics from the University of Chicago. His research agenda focuses on dynamic contracting, government reputation, game theory, and macroeconomics, the Minneapolis Fed said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Iran says it has ‘received signs’ that that U.S. is ready to stop the blockade – report
data.description:
Iran’s UN Ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani said Tuesday that negotiations with the U.S. will resume as soon as the naval blockade ends, calling the blockade a violation of the ceasefire, AP News reported.

“I think the next round of the negotiations will take place in Islamabad,” the ambassador said, reiterating that lifting the blockade is a condition for new talks.

When asked about the possibility of military escalation, Iravani signaled Iran’s readiness for either outcome.

“If they want to go to the war, in this case also Iran is ready for that,” he told reporters at U.N. headquarters in New York, though he added, “We should give the chance—we hope.”

The warning came as a two-week truce is set to expire Wednesday, with last-minute ceasefire talks looking increasingly uncertain.

Vice President JD Vance’s trip to Pakistan for potential talks is on hold, according to a U.S. official, and Iran has not yet decided whether to join any new negotiations.

U.S. President Donald Trump told CNBC that he expects the U.S. to “end up with a great deal” with Iran to end the war. He also said he does not expect to extend the ongoing ceasefire, which will expire on Wednesday.

“I think they have no choice,” he said.

More on the U.S.-Iran war

  • Vance’s Islamabad trip on hold as Iran fails to respond to U.S. terms
  • JD Vance, U.S. delegation to land in Pakistan on Wednesday for talks with Iran- reports
  • Trump signals no extension of truce ahead of scheduled peace talks; oil down
  • Trump threatens again to blow up all Iran's bridges and power plants
  • When will Hormuz shipping normalize? See what traders are predicting

data.title: There is need for improvement in terms of centralization of Fed operations, says Fed's Waller
data.description:
There is a need to do more to centralize operations at the 12 Reserve Banks into national lines of business, according to Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller.

Waller was speaking on "Modernizing Reserve Bank Operations" before the Brookings Institution.

"I see no reason to reduce the number of Reserve Banks or alter their geographic boundaries. Each Bank president still has an independent voice at the FOMC on the appropriate course of monetary policy, and that should continue," he said.

"At the beginning, everything a Reserve Bank did was 'local'—no national functions were performed," said Waller.

"This decentralized approach made more sense when the economy and the banking system were much more regional in nature, but as finance and the economy became more national in scope, changes were needed," added the Fed governor.

"In this era, Reserve Banks operated under a clear 'Bank first, System second' mindset," he noted.

"Over the decades, as technology changed and U.S. financial sector regulation evolved, the external environment began to shift in important ways," Waller said.

"The Reserve Banks saw a significant reduction in operations and employment at their branches, to the point of closing and selling some buildings. Even at head offices, the automation of check processing and other labor-intensive payments work reduced manpower needs and employment," said Waller.

Citing examples of centralization, Waller cited Fed financial services consolidated into a single national payment service line and the Fed's role as fiscal agent for the Treasury, handling payments and securities issuance, auctions, and redemptions, as well as providing other banking services.

"Despite this gradual movement toward more centralization in payments, IT, and fiscal agency, many support functions, including HR, procurement, and finance, are still run more or less separately at each of the 12 Reserve Banks. In my view, we have reached a point where we need to better exploit the efficiency and risk reduction benefits of standardizing and probably centrally leading all of these functions," said Waller.

With these functions, the Fed's philosophy must be "System first, Bank second," according to the Fed governor.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: Autodesk remains mostly insulated from AI disruption, Middle East conflict: Oppenheimer
data.description:
Autodesk's (ADSK) business remains mostly insulated from the persistent AI disruption narrative, and its diversification strategy should also help it more easily navigate the current conflict in the Middle East, according to Oppenheimer.

"Regarding AI, we were reminded that Autodesk's advantages of proprietary data, end-market context, IP/data permissions, and critical data/IP safeguards will be difficult for startups to replicate," said Oppenheimer analysts, led by Ken Wong, in an investor note.

Instead, Autodesk views AI as a tailwind, as it can help modernize the architecture, engineering, and construction technology stack.

Although the AI disruption narrative might not affect its revenue, Autodesk shares have declined 17% year to date.

"Autodesk isn't immune to macro shocks, catering to end markets sensitive to the economy," Wong noted. "However, ADSK's diversification provides some resiliency. The Middle East accounts for a mid-single-digit percentage of revenue, and the business is well diversified across geographies, end markets, and customer sizes. If macro risks escalate, we see potential for management to de-risk the full-year outlook, similar to FY26 guidance."

Oppenheimer maintains its Outperform rating and $325 price target on the stock.

"We expect Autodesk to deliver durable high-teens to low 20% earnings growth powered by HSD-LDD revenue growth and consistent margin expansion in the mid- to long term," Wong added. "We also believe Autodesk is well positioned to capitalize on the digitization of the construction market, which remains a large ($12B TAM) analog end-market."

data.title: A smoking ban in the UK for people born after 2008 becomes a reality
data.description:
The UK's House of Commons and the House of Lords agreed on the final text of the highly watched Tobacco and Vapes Bill. The legislation creates what the government calls a “smokefree generation” by making it permanently illegal to sell tobacco products to anyone born on or after January 1, 2009. That cutoff means anyone currently 17 or younger will never legally be able to purchase cigarettes, cigars, herbal smoking products, or cigarette papers in the UK. The legal age of sale will then rise by one year each year starting in 2027, so the restriction expands permanently forward in time.

Government ministers will gain new powers under the legislation to regulate the flavors, packaging, and advertising of vaping and other consumer nicotine products, with an eye toward preventing products from being marketed to appeal to children. Adults who buy vaping or nicotine products on behalf of minors will also face fines. Retail licensing requirements for nicotine product sellers are expected as well. The bill now heads to royal assent to become law, which is now a constitutional formality rather than a real veto sticking point. 

The tobacco companies that will be most impacted by the sweeping tobacco bill are Imperial Brands (IMBBY) (IMBBF) and British American Tobacco (BTI) (BTAFF), given their combined dominance of UK cigarette sales. Japan Tobacco International (JAPAY) (JAPAF) and Philip Morris International (PM) will see their long-term UK combustible volumes structurally eroded as the generational ban takes hold.

data.title: Amazon One Medical launches GLP-1 management program
data.description:
• Members of the Amazon (AMZN) One Medical primary care health service, as well as cash-pay customers, can access obesity medications such as Novo Nordisk's (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) and Eli Lilly's (LLY) Zepbound (tirzepatide) and Foundayo (orforglipron) through a new GLP-1 management program.
  • The offering provides users with primary care, pharmacy services, and virtual care options to support weight loss. 
  • Under the program, individuals receive a pre-visit screening, structured consultations, regular follow-up visits with monitoring, documentation, and evidence-based treatment algorithms that include built-in safety protocols. 
  • Cash-pay customers will pay as low as $299 for injectable meds and as low as $149 a month for pills through Amazon Pharmacy.

data.title: Ramaco Resources on the rise as Goldman upgrades after stock's YTD weakness
data.description:
Ramaco Resources (METC) up 8.1% in Tuesday's trading as Goldman Sachs upgraded the metallurgical coal producer to Neutral from Sell with a $15 price target, ticked up from $14, pointing to increasing met coal assumptions on improved market dynamics.

Goldman's Brian Lee said his Neutral rating is supported by Ramaco's (METC) valuation coming down to more palpable levels, with the analyst attributing the weakness to operational shifts in its rare earths business, which he had initially viewed as causing the company to be relatively overvalued.

More importantly, Ramaco's (METC) strategic shift within its rare earths segment has the company looking to de-emphasize its reliance on scandium production, which was a key part of Goldman's Sell thesis, Lee wrote.

Additionally, the analyst said dynamics within the coal industry have seen some recent improvements, which he now believes can support a more rapid expansion of its coal assets and drive improved EBITDA generation.

As a result, Lee said he views Ramaco's (METC) continued share price decline as providing less downside potential, supported by its core coal business.
